tags.name: Air Wall
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Wall dive on east/southwest Gili Air, drops to 30m. Gorgonian sea fans confirmed. Bumphead parrotfish confirmed by multiple sources (trawanganstingraydivers.com description). Depth corrected from 22m to 30m.

tags.name: Bounty
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12 RE-AUDIT: Vessel is a pontoon/floating pier for Gili Meno jetty construction. Sank in 2001 storm. Depth range: 8-18m (divecentralgili.com), 9-18m (oceans5dive.com). One outlier source (trawanganstingraydivers.com) claims 25-26m — likely error, majority sources confirm 18m max. Confirmed species: frogfish/angler fish, scorpionfish, drummer fish, sweetlips. Pygmy seahorses claimed by divecentralgili.com/trawangandive.com but not confirmed universally — retained as possible but flagged. Oceans5dive.com also notes coral-covered bicycles at south end (unusual).

tags.name: Deep Turbo
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Pinnacle on northeast Gili Trawangan, sandy bottom at 30m with sea mounds/pinnacles. gilidivers.com confirms pinnacle type, depth 18-30m. trawangandive.com confirms deep pinnacle with strong currents. Confirmed species: reef sharks, stingrays, nudibranchs, barrel sponges, soft corals. gilidivers.com adds pygmy seahorses and redtooth triggerfish schools.

tags.name: Frog Fish Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Macro site on east Gili Air. Depth 5-18m. Confirmed species: frogfish, ghost pipefish, scorpionfish, garden eels, clownfish (diversiondivetravel.com.au). Seahorses mentioned by starfish.ch as 'Gili Air Harbour/Frogfish Point'. Previous markdown claimed 'warty frogfish, giant frogfish, hairy frogfish' as three confirmed species — only 'frogfish' is generically confirmed; specific species breakdown unverified and removed.

tags.name: Halik
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: North side of Gili Trawangan. Drift reef, 5-24m. NOT same as Trawangan Wall (separate site on northwest). divezone.net confirms location 'northern side', features bumphead parrotfish and eagle rays. gilidivers.com confirms large antler coral bommies, baby whitetip reef sharks, cuttlefish, moray eels, ribbon eels, blue-spotted stingrays. Depth corrected from 20m to 24m.

tags.name: Jack Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: North side of Gili Trawangan. Two coral ridges with white sand at 24-35m+. manta-dive-giliair.com tags as Advanced, depth 24-35m. Weather/current dependent. Marine life: big-eye trevally schools, whitetip and blacktip reef sharks, eagle rays, devil rays, snappers. Difficulty corrected from Intermediate to Advanced. Depth corrected from 20m to 35m. Site type kept as drift — current-driven dive.

tags.name: Malang
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Takat Malang, northeast of Gili Meno. gilidivers.com confirms ~10 Takats from 12m plateau to 40m. bluemarlindive.com confirms 20m avg/40m max, overhangs, outcrops, swim-throughs. Confirmed species: blue-spotted stingrays, garden eels, cuttlefish, kuhls (gilidivers.com). Previous markdown claimed 'very large gorgonian sea fans' — confirmed by multiple sources including bluemarlindive.com and starfish.ch search results. Depth 40m retained.

tags.name: Manta Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Also called Sunset Reef. Sloping reef 5-24m. CRITICAL CORRECTION: Manta ray sightings only 3-4 times since 2013 (terumbudivers.com). Mobula rays far more common. Previous markdown said 'seasonal manta visits' which is technically true but deeply misleading. Confirmed species: green/hawksbill turtles, Oriental sweetlips, batfish, cuttlefish, lionfish, morays, gorgonian fans. Depth corrected from 20m to 24m.

tags.name: Meno Corner
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: No specific sources found naming this site as 'Meno Corner'. Most operator sites describe Gili Meno dive sites as: Turtle Heaven/City (NE side), Meno Wall, Meno Slope, Bounty Wreck. 'Meno Corner' may be a local name for the northeast tip area near Turtle Heaven. Cannot validate with 3+ independent sources — left unvalidated. Previous specific depth claims ('pinnacle 9-30m') are unverified.

tags.name: Meno Slope
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Sandy slope with large boulders on Gili Meno. oceans5dive.com confirms 20m max (Beginner-Intermediate). trawanganstingraydivers.com: 28m max, turtles, eels, scorpionfish, night dive site, underwater statue (two couples hugging). Type 'reef' is correct (slope). Confirmed species: hawksbill and green turtles, blue-striped snappers (oceans5dive.com), turtles, eels, scorpionfish (trawanganstingraydivers.com). Depth corrected from 30m to 28m.

tags.name: Meno Wall
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Northwest Gili Meno, wall dive. oceans5dive.com: 25m max, excellent for night diving, crustaceans, Spanish dancers, scorpionfish. trawanganstingraydivers.com: 24m max, seahorse, clownfish, angelfish. bluemarlindive.com: 14m avg/22m max, soft corals. Confirmed marine life: moray eels, scorpionfish, lionfish (oceans5dive.com), pygmy seahorses (trawanganstingraydivers.com), lobsters at night. Bamboo sharks were in previous markdown — NOT confirmed by any source, removed. Depth corrected from 22m to 25m.

tags.name: Mirkos Reef
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Long seamount/ridge between Gili Air and Gili Meno. 12-35m. manta-dive-giliair.com confirms: patch reef, ridges from 35m to 18m, flat top at 12m. Confirmed species: green sea turtles, fusiliers, snappers, blue-spotted ribbon-tailed rays, giant frogfish, white-tip reef sharks (manta-dive-giliair.com). Also listed as 'Secret Reef' by some operators. M-statue placed in memory of a diver named Mirkos.

tags.name: Shallow Turbo
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Northeast Gili Trawangan. Shallow end of Deep Turbo site. trawanganstingraydivers.com confirms 'shallow end of Deep Turbo' with 18m max, sandy bottom with coral heads. Confirmed species: pufferfish, batfish, sea turtles, garden eels, ghost pipefish, unicorn fish. Depth 18m confirmed. Difficulty Beginner is appropriate.

tags.name: Shark Point
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Northwest Gili Trawangan. divecentralgili.com confirms Glenn Nusa tugboat intentionally sunk March 2016. terumbudivers.com confirms February 2016, 22m long, 28m depth. gilidivers.com confirms 'Glen Nusa Ship Wreck' at Shark Point, sunk 2016. Confirmed marine life: blacktip and whitetip reef sharks, green turtles, sweetlips, snappers. Previous markdown claimed 'Glenn Nusa' at depth 25m (from Shark Point markdown) — actual depth is ~28m. Depth of main site corrected from 25m to 30m.

tags.name: Simons Reef
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Between Gili Meno and Gili Air (not 'north of Gili Islands' as previous markdown claimed). divezone.net: 25-40m, sandy bottom at 35m, Advanced only, sea mounds, overhangs, pinnacle. oceans5dive.com: 30m max, 'great variety of corals not seen on other dive sites', pinnacle with tropical fish. bluemarlindive.com: 25m avg/40m max, eight major seamounts, pygmy seahorse sightings. Depth corrected to 35m (consistent across majority of sources). Previous claim of 'large canyons' should be 'sea mounds/bommies with overhangs'.

tags.name: Segaluh
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: No specific sources found for a dive site named 'Segaluh' in the Lombok/Gili Islands area. This may be a local Lombok mainland reef site not covered by major Gili Islands operators. Cannot validate without 3+ independent sources. Left unvalidated.

tags.name: Teluk NBoro
tags.note: source is Diveschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: No specific sources found for 'Teluk NBoro' as a named dive site. Coordinates place it on Lombok's coast south of Gili Islands. Previous markdown linked it to 'Mentigi Bay seahorse population' — this claim is unverified and based on regional inference, not site-specific sources. Seahorse claims removed from markdown. Cannot validate without 3+ independent sources.

tags.name: Trawangan Wall
tags.note: source is Diverschool Notice Board - Location might have to be checked with a GPS
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: Northwest Gili Trawangan. CORRECTION: This is NOT the same site as Halik (which is on the north side). Trawangan Wall is a distinct site on the northwest with a deep drop-off exceeding 50m. Confirmed: schools of jackfish, sharks, tugboat wreck at 30m (likely Glenn Nusa), shallow turtle zone at 5m. Previous markdown described it as 'Halik Reef' — this was incorrect. Marine life: surgeon fish schools, turtles in sea grass, reef sharks. Depth corrected from 22m to 50m (the wall drops to 50m+). Difficulty updated to Advanced for the wall sections.

tags.name: Lombok Dive
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: No specific sources found for a dive site named 'Lombok Dive'. This may be a generic location marker. Cannot validate without 3+ independent sources.

tags.name: Vila Almarick Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: East coast Gili Trawangan. Gili Eco Trust Biorock programme started 2004 (first installation 20 November 2004 per Wikipedia), NOT 'since 2006' as previous markdowns stated. 110+ structures as of 2016 (Wikipedia), not 120+. Named after Vila Almarik resort. Depth corrected from 18m to 15m (structures cluster 5-15m). Structure names confirmed across multiple operators.

tags.name: Bale Sampan Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: East coast Gili Trawangan. Biorock programme started 2004. 110+ structures (2016 data). Depth corrected from 18m to 15m.

tags.name: Samba Vilas Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: East coast Gili Trawangan. Biorock programme started 2004. 110+ structures (2016 data). Depth corrected from 18m to 15m.

tags.name: Trawangan Dive Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: East coast Gili Trawangan, Trawangan Dive house reef. Biorock programme started 2004. Depth corrected from 18m to 15m.

tags.name: Gili Divers Biorock
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: East coast Gili Trawangan. Biorock programme started 2004. gilidivers.com confirms pipefish, juvenile frogfish, flatworms, crustaceans on the structures. Depth corrected from 18m to 15m.

tags.name: Turtle Point
tags.notes: QA 2026-06-12: NE Gili Meno (multiple sources confirm Gili Meno, not Gili Trawangan). Pinnacle from 10m to 35m. gilidivers.com confirms pinnacle type, 10-35m, hawksbill and green turtles, nudibranchs, pipefish, frogfish. trawangandive.com: Hawksbill and green turtles, cuttlefish. oceans5dive.com: 30m max, all levels, turtles. Site type corrected from reef to pinnacle. Difficulty Beginner retained — site is described as 'all levels' by multiple operators; upper section 10-18m is genuinely beginner-accessible. '10 turtles per dive' claim from Manta Dive: presented as 'if you are lucky', not routine.
